title: selinux/podman issue for rootless pod: error while loading shared libraries: /lib64/libc.so.6: cannot apply  ....
body:
### Issue Description

I set up a fresh fedora f39 server in order to get some rootless podman on my new intel server. The server installation went on a small ssd, where the technical users, that will run the rootless-container  services using podman are located on a raid-mount. The technical users have been set up using the cockpit-ui.  They techuser are located under '/mnt/raider/home2/techuser01'.

# The problem looks like this:
```
$ podman  run -it --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest
/bin/bash: error while loading shared libraries: /lib64/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
```
# Here is a list of the installed components ( Fedora-Server F39 is updated frequently)
- podman-4.9.4-1.fc39.x86_64
- podman info gives me:  (see following text field

# What i tried in the meantime
@rhatdan wrote, that all selinux related problems with shared libs are configuration problems. Yes i disabled selinux and the problem disappeard. The i tried the proposed fix/workarounds  described in 
- #3234  using the semanage and restorecon [moved container storage ... no it was not but i tried](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/3234#issuecomment-497541854)
- #10817 some removals of my '...containers/*' directory and a `podman system reset`  

I have no idea where to look now, i am done. The only thing i am thinking of is a betes-noir i configured with the '/mnt/raider'  mount.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a technical user on a mounted raid drive
2. with this tech-user issue the call 'podman  run -it --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest'
3.


### Describe the results you received

/bin/bash: error while loading shared libraries: /lib64/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied

### Describe the results you expected

a running shell or test

comments.body: when you revisit this, could you change the title to reflect more of the problem than it currently does?  thanks in advance for due consideration.

This is an SELinux issue. 

You used an alternate directory for container content, not in a standard directory.

https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/main/docs/containers-storage.conf.5.md#storage-table

Did you fix the SELinux labels to work correctly?

You could also submit the AVC messages.

sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent.

Salut 

For the first: i did not changed directory of the container content. I simply used that one, that was given by the created account. But ..  it must have been my fault. I created two accounts:

- techuser01 that resides of the boot drive (ssd)  
- techuser02 that resides on the mounted raid

For both cases i tested the above shown command `podman  run -it --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest`  and both worked! So it is my fault an i will close that issue. I guess that the used techaccount was botched by myself. 
So i will drop the techuser and recreate it.

Anyway i will have to undo the system modification for that user, that may disturb future actions taken place on a newly created account, that uses the same account name.  So ...


List all saved selinux settings of the related tech-users
```
$ sudo semanage fcontext -l | grep techlxoffice
/mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage(/.*)?
... and more
```

and drop 'em step by step
```
$ sudo semanage fcontext -d  /mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage
... and more
```

I'll try this again and on success i will document it and will close that issue. Thank you for your time!

UPDATE:
I cleaned up the selinux settings and dropped an recreated the technical user account, and ... it worked.
_One thing I did differently_:  i used the real path to the homedir of the technical user and i avoided any
use of symbolic links to shorten a path. I guess that might be the reason, that messed up the sec-handing.
